zh.Caen, Calvados, Normandy, France: 法國諾門迪卡爾瓦多斯凱
zh.lifeSummary: Harold de Vaux Pentney de Gouvis於1065年出生在法國諾門迪卡爾瓦多斯凱。他於1090年與Beatrice Muncheny在法國諾門迪卡爾瓦多斯凱結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Harold
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from here ‘army’ + weald ‘ruler’. In pre-Conquest England, this was reinforced by the related Old Norse name Haraldr, introduced by Scandinavian settlers. The name was not at all popular in England after the Conquest, probably because of its association with the unfortunate King Harold , killed at the Battle of Hastings in 1066 . It was used in some parts of Nottinghamshire in the 16th and 17th centuries, and revived more generally, along with a number of other Old English names, in the 19th century, when it suddenly became extremely popular.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

nameMeanings.name: Vaux
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of various places in northern France called Vaux, from the Old French plural of val ‘valley’. Compare English Vause and French Devaux .

History: Richard Vaux (1749–90) emigrated from Sussex, England, to Philadelphia, PA, in early youth. Calvert Vaux, landscape architect, emigrated from London, England, to America in 1850 and designed several major US parks, including Central Park in New York City (with Frederick Olmstead). — This surname is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors (along with its variant Veaux and altered form Devoe ) and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America.

nameMeanings.name: Pentney
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: locative name from Pentney (Norfolk), which is recorded as Pentenay in 1200. The place-name may derive from an Old English personal name * Penta (genitive * Pentan) or a Celtic river-name Pante (probably from pant ‘hollow’) + Old English ēg ‘island’.

en.lifeSummary: Harold de Vaux Pentney de Gouvis was born in 1065, in Caen, Calvados, Normandy, France. He married Beatrice Muncheny in 1090, in Caen, Calvados, Normandy, France. They were the parents of at least 1 son.

fr.lifeSummary: Harold de Vaux Pentney de Gouvis est né en 1065, à Caen, Calvados, Normandie, France. Il a épousé Beatrice Muncheny en 1090, à Caen, Calvados, Normandie, France. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils.

de.lifeSummary: Harold de Vaux Pentney de Gouvis wurde 1065 in Caen, Calvados, Normandie, Frankreich geboren. Er hat 1090 in Caen, Calvados, Normandie, Frankreich Beatrice Muncheny geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn.

it.lifeSummary: Harold de Vaux Pentney de Gouvis è nato il nel 1065 nel Caen, Calvados, Normandie, Francia. Lui sposato Beatrice Muncheny nel 1090, nel Caen, Calvados, Normandie, Francia. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio.

ko.lifeSummary: Harold de Vaux Pentney de Gouvis(은)는 1065년 프랑스 노먼디 캘바도스 카이에서 태어났다. 그는 1090년 프랑스 노먼디 캘바도스 카이에서 Beatrice Muncheny(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Harold de Vaux Pentney de Gouvis nasceu em 1065, em Caen, Calvados, Normandie, França. Ele casou-se com Beatrice Muncheny em 1090, em Caen, Calvados, Normandie, França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho.

ru.lifeSummary: Harold de Vaux Pentney de Gouvis родился в 1065 г. в местности каен, калвадос, норманди, Франция. Он женился на женщине по имени Beatrice Muncheny в 1090 г. в местности каен, калвадос, норманди, Франция. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын.

es.lifeSummary: Harold de Vaux Pentney de Gouvis nació en 1065, en Caen, Calvados, Normandie, Francia. Se casó con Beatrice Muncheny en 1090, en Caen, Calvados, Normandie, Francia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo.
